free_text: Isotopic mixing  in  the isobaric  analogue states  of
The measurements of the photoproton energy  spectrum,
the total photoneutron cross section and the neutron
spectrum to the  first  state  Zr-89 were  made. The
correlations  between  the  isotopic  analogue state
detected in the photoproton spectrum  and  structure
observed  in  the  total  photoneutron cross section
were obtained.
Data  were compiled In  SCJAD of  Saratov
State University.
Corrected  by V.Varlamov,  the Russia MSU
SINP CDFE: REFERENCE, ERR-ANALYS, REACTION in SUBENT
002,  BRS to BRA  in  SUBENT  004, datas, lowercase,
typographical mistakes, EN-MAX deleted and E-EXC ->
EN in SUBENT 002, etc.
Proton counts divided by bremsstrahlung spectrum.
The 5 mg/cm**2 thick foil of  zirconium  enriched  to
97.65% by Zr-90 was used.
SI(LI-detectors of 3.1 mm thickness were used.
Statistical errors of measurements.
A significant  feature  of the spectrum is the double
resonance with peaks at 19.0 MeV and 19.35 MeV.  The
higher   component  is  identified  as  an  isotopic
analogue state.
The 67.5 g weight target of Zr-90 oxide was used.
4PI    Halpern-type    neutron    detector
consisted  of  sixteen  BF3  detectors  in  paraffin
moderator  were  arranged  in  two  concentric rings
around the  sample  so  that  the  system  detection
efficiency was independent of neutron energy.
Eight  independent  yield   curves   were
obtained  in 100 keV steps from threshold to 28 MeV.
the cross section  was  extracted  from  these  data
using a variable bin Penfold-Leiss technique.
Root-mean-squared errors.
Multiplicity correction  of  total photoneutron cross
section was made.
Method for neutron multiplicity sorting correction.
The splitting of the giant resonance into  two  peaks
at 16.2 MeV and 17.2 MeV was observed.
The difference spectrum.
The 67.5 g weight target of Zr-90 oxide was used.
The spectrometer consisted of 5 cm diameter by
2.5 cm thick stilbene crystal.
Collection of recoild protons.
Statistical errors of measurements.
The difference   neutron   spectrum  was  obtained  by
subtraction of photoneutron spectrum produced by  the
20  MeV  bremsstrahlung from the spectrum produced by
the 24 MeV bremsstrahlung.
